title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime
description: An embedded controller caches addresses across callbacks while automatic, static, thread, and allocated objects begin and end at different times. Learners cumulatively produce object lifetime and address ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.
Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.
Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.
